questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.To play in a happy and noisy way
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Romp”.Romp: (used about children and animals) to play in a happy and noisy way. Strut: to walk in proud way. Prance: to move about with quick, high steps, often because you feel proud or pleased with yourself. Amble: to walk at a slow relaxed speed.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Obdurate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “adamant”.Obdurate: stubbornly refusing to change one’s opinion or course of action (syn = adamant). Angry: feeling or showing strong annoyance, displeasure, or hostility; full of anger. Adamant: refusing to be persuaded or to change one’s mind. Obsessed: preoccupy or fill the mind of (someone) continually and to a troubling extent. Calm: no feeling of nervousness, anger, or other strong emotions.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.Give the pregnant woman a nourishing diet.
options: The pregnant woman must have given a nourishing diet.
The pregnant woman was given a nourishing diet.
The pregnant woman should be given a nourishing diet.
The pregnant women is given a nourishing diet.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “The pregnant woman should be given a nourishing diet”.The given sentence is an example of an imperative sentence. Subject + V1 + object (active voice) ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742024699196_image001.png) object + should + be + V3 + subject (passive voice) Hence, the correct answer is “The pregnant woman should be given a nourishing diet”.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.Did you cooked / the tomato basil penne pasta / yourself this time?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Did you cooked”.Here, ‘Did you cook’ should be used instead of ‘Did you cooked’. Did is an auxiliary verb and after auxiliary verb “base form of the verb” is used. Therefore, the correct answer is option B.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.COARSE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Smooth”.Coarse: consisting of large pieces; rough, not smooth. Thick: with opposite sides or surfaces that are far or relatively far apart. Rough: having an uneven or irregular surface; not smooth or level. Smooth: having an even and regular surface. Crude: Simple and not skillfully done or made. (rude and offensive).

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Relevent”.The correct spell is “relevant” which means closely connected or appropriate to what is being done or considered. Retort: say something in answer to a remark, typically in a sharp, angry, or witty manner. Revenge: the action of hurting or harming someone in return for an injury or wrong suffered at their hands. Restaurant: a place where meals are prepared and served to customers.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Did you / recall to meet / him at the event / last night?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “recall to meet”. Here, ‘recall meeting’ should be used instead of ‘recall to meet’. There are some verbs which is followed by a to-infinitive (e.g., to run), while others require a gerund (e.g., going). Gerunds are derived from verbs. They end in- ing and act as nouns. In the given sentence the verb ‘recall’ will be followed by a gerund. Thus, the correct sentence is “Did you recall meeting him at the event last night.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Reticent
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “taciturn”.Reticent: unwilling to speak about your thoughts or feelings. Taciturn: reserved or uncommunicative in speech. Lively: full of life and energy. Loquacious: tending to talk a great deal Verbose: using or expressed in more words than are needed.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.Chink in one’s armour
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “weak point”.Chink in one’s armour: weak point where someone can be attacked.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.feeling inside you which tells you what is right and what is wrong
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Conscience”.Conscience: a person’s moral sense of right and wrong. Clever: quick to understand, learn, and devise or apply ideas, intelligent. Consciousness: the state of being aware of and responsive to one’s surroundings. Examination: a detailed inspection or study.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.A. As a result, they are more able to live in harmony with themselves and with the world that surrounds them. B. Stabilizing meditation is catalogued as one of the hardest techniques of meditation because followers must keep their minds under total control for long period of time. C. The benefits of this meditation, according to the followers, is that meditators can experience full understanding of their thoughts and ideas. D. This consists of maintaining full attention to their role object focus.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “BDCA”.The author is talking about meditation. So, the correct sequence is BDCA. B. Stabilizing meditation is catalogued as one of the hardest techniques of meditation because followers must keep their minds under total control for long period of time. D. This consists of maintaining full attention to their role object focus. C. The benefits of this meditation, according to the followers, is that meditators can experience full understanding of their thoughts and ideas. A. As a result, they are more able to live in harmony with themselves and with the world that surrounds them.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.The business prospect seemed quite _______ so I convinced my brother to partner with me to start the venture.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “lucrative”.Lucrative: producing a great deal of profit. Flourishing: developing rapidly and successfully; thriving. Dubious: hesitating or doubtful. Prosperous: successful, usually by earning a lot of money.

questions.question: Select the option that gives the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. The district magistrate of Jaipur takes care of the rank and file in the district.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “rank and file”. Rank and file: ordinary workers in a company or the ordinary members of an organization.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the BOLD segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’.Mixed cropping should be encouraged both as a hedge for price risk and for its ecological benefits.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “hedge against”.Hedge against: to do something to protect yourself against problems, especially against losing money. Hedge in: to surround or restrict (someone) in a way that prevents free movement or action.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.Public attention in the developed world has / recently focused on asthma because of its / rapidly increasing prevalence, / effecting up to one in four urban children.
options: recently focused on asthma because of its
effecting up to one in four urban children
Public attention in the developed world has
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “effecting up to one in four urban children”.the given sentence is grammatically correct. “has recently focused” the adverb of frequency is used between the auxiliary verb and the main verb. “Effecting” should be changed into “affecting”. Effect(noun): it is most commonly used as a result and consequence. Effect is always visible or we can say it is tangible. Affect(verb): mean to influence someone or something, rather than to cause something. Affect is abstract feeling.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Laceration
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Healing”.Laceration: a deep cut or tear in skin or flesh Healing: the process of making or becoming sound or healthy again. Recuperation: recovery from illness or exertion. Convalescence: time spent recovering from an illness or medical treatment (recuperation). Palpitation: a noticeably, rapid, strong, or irregular heartbeat due to agitation, exertion, or illness.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the BOLD segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’.The Chairman send in all the right signals and we can only hope that this will result in rich dividends.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “send out”.Send in: to order somebody to go to a place to deal with a difficult situation. Send out: produce, emit, or give out something. Send of: there is no such phrasal verb. Send off: instruct someone to go.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.Progress in life depends / a good deal under crossing / one threshold / after another.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “a good deal under crossing”.Depend + on/upon. Hence, the correct sentence is “Progress in life depends a good deal upon crossing one threshold after another”.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.Shikha gifted me a beautiful handbag.
options: I have been gifted a beautiful handbag by Shikha.
I was gifted a beautiful handbag by Shikha.
I am being gifted a beautiful handbag by Shikha.
I am gifted a beautiful handbag by Shikha.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “I am being gifted a beautiful handbag by Shikha”.Simple past tense: Subject + V3 + object ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742024699196_image001.png) object + was/ were + V3 + by + subject Shikha gifted me a beautiful handbag I was gifted a beautiful handbag by Shikha

questions.question: Select the correct indirect speech form of the given sentence.Mother said to me, “I’m worried about your career.”
options: Mother told me that she was worried about my career.
Mother told me that she had been worried about my career.
Mother said she is worried about your career.
Mother said that I was worrying about your career.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Mother told me that she was worried about my career”.‘Said to’ change into ‘told’. Comma and inverted commas will be removed. The conjunction ‘that’ will be added. ‘I’ will be changed into ‘she’ and ‘am’ will be changed into ‘was’. Hence, correct sentence “Mother told me that she was worried about my career”.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank.The fire season that started in late 2019 in Australia (1) ______ massive devastation and things swiftly got worse before rains finally came and helped contain the (2) ______ fires ever. The smoke released by the fires became (3) ______ disaster. On January 1st, Australia’s capital recorded the worst pollution it’s ever seen, with an air quality index 23 times higher (4) ______ what’s considered ‘hazardous. Smoke in the city crept into birthing rooms, stopped MRI machines from working, and (5) ______ a heart attack in one elderly woman who died soon after she stepped off a plane. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “caused”.Caused: make (something, especially something bad) happen. Faced: having a face or expression of a specified kind. Planned: decide on and make arrangements for in advance. Wanted: have a desire to possess or do (something)

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank.The fire season that started in late 2019 in Australia (1) ______ massive devastation and things swiftly got worse before rains finally came and helped contain the (2) ______ fires ever. The smoke released by the fires became (3) ______ disaster. On January 1st, Australia’s capital recorded the worst pollution it’s ever seen, with an air quality index 23 times higher (4) ______ what’s considered ‘hazardous. Smoke in the city crept into birthing rooms, stopped MRI machines from working, and (5) ______ a heart attack in one elderly woman who died soon after she stepped off a plane. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “worst”.There is superlative degree of ‘bad’ will be used here.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank.The fire season that started in late 2019 in Australia (1)______ massive devastation and things swiftly got worse before rains finally came and helped contain the (2)______ fires ever. The smoke released by the fires became (3)______ disaster. On January 1st, Australia’s capital recorded the worst pollution it’s ever seen, with an air quality index 23 times higher (4)______ what’s considered ‘hazardous’. Smoke in the city crept into birthing rooms, stopped MRI machines from working, and (5)______ a heart attack in one elderly woman who died soon after she stepped off a plane. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “another”.Here is the most appropriate answer is ‘another’.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank.The fire season that started in late 2019 in Australia (1) ______ massive devastation and things swiftly got worse before rains finally came and helped contain the (2) ______ fires ever. The smoke released by the fires became (3) ______ disaster. On January 1st, Australia’s capital recorded the worst pollution it’s ever seen, with an air quality index 23 times higher (4) ______ what’s considered ‘hazardous. Smoke in the city crept into birthing rooms, stopped MRI machines from working, and (5) ______ a heart attack in one elderly woman who died soon after she stepped off a plane. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Than”. There is the most appropriate answer is “Than”.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The fire season that started in late 2019 in Australia (1) ______ massive devastation and things swiftly got worse before rains finally came and helped contain the (2) ______ fires ever. The smoke released by the fires became (3) ______ disaster. On January 1st, Australia’s capital recorded the worst pollution it’s ever seen, with an air quality index 23 times higher (4) ______ what’s considered ‘hazardous. Smoke in the city crept into birthing rooms, stopped MRI machines from working, and (5) ______ a heart attack in one elderly woman who died soon after she stepped off a plane. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “triggered”.There is third form of verb is used. Hence, C is the correct answer.
